name: bosch-cnc

env_axis.name: machine-and-time
env_axis.shift_mechanism: cross-machine (M1+M2 → M3) AND within-machine temporal drift across 2-year span (Oct 2018 – Aug 2021); also cross-process within machine

facts: 3 CNC mills × 15 machining programs × 6×6-month windows (Oct 2018–Aug 2021) of triaxial accelerometer + binary anomaly label.
hypotheses: Cross-machine shift (M1+M2 → M3) is a categorically different shift type from within-machine 2-year temporal drift, because the two are not factorable through a single hidden 'machine state' environment.
The 6-month window carries mechanism (tool-wear cycles) rather than merely data-collection cadence, because wear accumulates on a comparable timescale.
intuitions: Follow-ups to Tnani 2022 CIRP for 2023-25 and SSMSPC extensions likely exist.
Industrial-AI work distinguishing brownfield drift from greenfield deployment probably uses this dataset.
unknowns: The 'machining process' boundary is fixed by program-identity in the data, so whether a process is a labelled unit or a discovered segmentation of the time series cannot be settled here.
Tool-wear is plausibly both a process (mechanism) and a temporal (sequence) phenomenon; the accelerometer alone cannot decide between them.
epistemic_state.boundary: With Ty=1 (accelerometer only, single modality) and no temperature/current/acoustic complement, the dataset caps what typed shift it can show.

representational_commitments: Machining-program-identity defines process boundary (not discovered from signal)
6-month windowing reflects data-collection cadence, not necessarily mechanism cycle
Binary anomaly label collapses fault types
